disclaimer: Rubriker och utdrag kommer från angivna källor. Agent-sammanfattningar ska faktagranskas mot källänken.

items.title: The Coast Guard rescued a loggerhead sea turtle after an anchor line mangled her flipper. See her incredible 4-week recovery
items.source_excerpt: In early June, the U.S. Coast Guard responded to an injured sea turtle off the coast of Florida.

items.title: Conservationists celebrate birth of baby pancake tortoise, hope for one of the world's most endangered reptiles
items.source_excerpt: The new hatchling, which will be named by the public, is the child of mother and father pancake tortoises Waffle and Maple.

items.title: Endangered West African leopards show signs of recovery: 'A rare piece of good news'
items.source_excerpt: Despite heavy conflict and rampant wildlife trafficking, new studies suggest cause for hope for the West African leopard population.

items.title: 172-pound loggerhead turtle with four fishing hooks in his body given second chance by volunteers
items.source_excerpt: Bowser, a 172-pound loggerhead sea turtle, was rescued from the Navarre Beach Fishing Pier recently. The operation was one of a growing number of rescues in areas where sea turtles and humans overlap.

items.title: This sea turtle was rescued with a missing flipper and a crushed shell. A special procedure saved his life
items.source_excerpt: Kraken, a green sea turtle, is in recovery after a harrowing boat strike in Abu Dhabi.

items.title: Baby 'sloth bear' found abandoned and injured on the forest floor given second chance by rescuers
items.source_excerpt: Experts say the young bear cub is now “responding well” to treatment.

items.title: Nepal's young birdwatchers are becoming conservationists one sighting at a time
items.source_excerpt: Nepal is home to more than 900 bird species, of which 40 are globally threatened and 172 are nationally threatened.

items.title: What went right this week: the good news that matters
items.source_excerpt: The world’s ‘greatest forest recovery’, a new test for womb cancer, and Europe’s environmental progress, plus more The post What went right this week: the good news that matters appeared first on Positive News .

items.title: Firefighters free raccoon trapped under sewer grate in 'jaws of life' rescue mission
items.source_excerpt: After the first responders had completed the job, the masked critter ran back to its natural habitat.

items.title: Fishermen save 220-pound loggerhead sea turtle from strangling ropes
items.source_excerpt: A daring sea turtle recovery mission in Australia was just the latest in a long list of sea turtle rescues.

items.title: I spent 6 months reading hundreds of poems written by teens. I was shocked to discover that they are full of hope
items.source_excerpt: Young people are facing a future shaped by climate anxiety, political extremism, economic instability, and chronic loneliness. But that's only part of the story.

items.title: This giant snail is the size of a croissant. Conservationists are banding together to save it from extinction
items.source_excerpt: The Obô Giant Snail is just one of many vulnerable species worth protecting in the “Galapagos of Africa.”

items.title: Volunteers read aloud and play music for shelter pets amid fireworks on July 4th
items.source_excerpt: Volunteers are gearing up for their sixth-annual “comfort sessions” at their local animal shelter.

items.title: Virginia fishermen fight 20 mph winds to rescue 700-pound leatherback from crab trap: 'I've never seen a turtle in distress like that'
items.source_excerpt: Three fishermen were cruising for fish in the Chesapeake Bay when they came across the biggest sea turtle species in the world.

items.title: A 250-pound sea turtle was found stranded on the highway, unable to move. She left rescuers with a single egg
items.source_excerpt: The Loggerhead desperately needed help getting back to the water. Rescuers got there just in time.

items.title: Jet ski riders conduct rescue mission for sea turtle with 'bloody, peeling' infection on her shell
items.source_excerpt: While enjoying a tour of Magnetic Island off the coast of Australia, the jet ski riders ended their outing early to help a turtle in need.

items.title: Critics doubted a $1.46B wildlife crossing. Trail cameras tell a success story: '75 recorded uses each night'
items.source_excerpt: The major wildlife corridor project includes 19 rope bridges, 24 underpasses, and two land bridges for local fauna.

items.title: Island conservationists celebrate 80X increase in nesting loggerhead turtles
items.source_excerpt: Globally, the loggerhead population has decreased by 47% over the past three generations.

items.title: After rewilding success, trail cameras captured the return of one of the Pacific Northwest’s most elusive mammals
items.source_excerpt: A rare ringtail was seen passing through a 405-acre property in Ashland, Oregon.

items.title: Conservationists 'ecstatic:' Trail cameras spot one of the world's rarest mammals — with a baby in its pouch
items.source_excerpt: Conservationists in Queensland, Australia “turned to mush upon seeing the images.”

items.title: This national park site is training volunteers to become 'sea turtle nest sitters'
items.source_excerpt: As turtle nesting season comes to a head, volunteers are needed to keep the endangered eggs safe.

items.title: A coyote pup wandered into a cactus and was barbed by hundreds of spikes. Rescuers saved his life
items.source_excerpt: The 3-pound pup was exploring a homeowner’s lawn in Surprise, Arizona, when he got trapped in a cactus’s grip.
